title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud services. By exploiting compromised CI/CD pipelines, attackers can deploy malicious code, potentially leading to data encryption, service disruptions, and financial losses.
recommended_actions: Immediately review and secure CI/CD pipelines by implementing strong authentication mechanisms, such as multi-factor authentication (MFA), and limiting access to critical systems.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions to monitor for suspicious activity in cloud environments and ensure regular backups are stored offline or in immutable storage.
Conduct employee training on phishing awareness and implement email filtering solutions to reduce the likelihood of initial compromise.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with low severity.

title: uBidAuction 2.0.1 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability in the auctions/manage...
description: uBidAuction 2.0.1 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability in the auctions/manage module. The date_created, date_from, date_to, and created_at parameters in the filter functionality are not properly sanitized, allowing remote attackers to inject malicious scripts via crafted GET requests that execute in victims' browsers.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2022-50968.

title: WordPress Plugin AAWP 3.16 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows...
description: WordPress Plugin AAWP 3.16 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows authenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts by manipulating the tab parameter. Attackers can craft URLs with XSS payloads in the tab parameter of the aawp-settings admin page to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the context of authenticated users.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in the context of authenticated users, potentially leading to session hijacking, data theft, or further exploitation within the WordPress admin panel.
recommended_actions: Update the AAWP plugin to the latest patched version if available.
Implement input validation and sanitization on the 'tab' parameter to prevent malicious script injection.
Restrict access to the WordPress admin panel to trusted IPs and ensure strong authentication mechanisms are in place.

title: WordPress Plugin Curtain 1.0.2 contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows...
description: WordPress Plugin Curtain 1.0.2 contains a cross-site request forgery vulnerability that allows attackers to activate or deactivate site maintenance mode by crafting malicious requests. Attackers can trick authenticated administrators into submitting forged requests to the options-general.php page with curtain parameters to toggle maintenance mode without valid nonce validation.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to exploit a lack of nonce validation in the WordPress Plugin Curtain 1.0.2, enabling them to toggle site maintenance mode without authorization. This can disrupt website availability and potentially harm user trust.
recommended_actions: Update the WordPress Plugin Curtain to the latest patched version or apply any available security updates from the plugin developer.
Implement additional CSRF protection mechanisms, such as verifying nonces on all sensitive actions.
Educate administrators to avoid clicking on untrusted links or performing administrative actions while logged into the WordPress dashboard.

title: WordPress Plugin amministrazione-aperta 3.7.3 contains a local file read vulnerability that...
description: WordPress Plugin amministrazione-aperta 3.7.3 contains a local file read vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to read arbitrary files by exploiting insufficient input validation in the open parameter. Attackers can supply file paths through the open GET parameter in dispatcher.php to include and read sensitive files accessible to the web server.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to read arbitrary files on the server, potentially exposing sensitive information such as configuration files, credentials, or user data. This could lead to further exploitation or compromise of the affected system.
recommended_actions: Immediately update the WordPress Plugin amministrazione-aperta to the latest patched version if available.
Implement web application firewall (WAF) rules to block malicious requests targeting the 'open' parameter in dispatcher.php.
Restrict file permissions on the web server to limit access to sensitive files and directories.

title: WordPress Contact Form Builder 1.6.1 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability that...
description: WordPress Contact Form Builder 1.6.1 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts by exploiting the form_id parameter. Attackers can craft malicious URLs to code_generator.php with script payloads in the form_id parameter to execute arbitrary JavaScript in victim browsers.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in victim browsers, potentially leading to session hijacking, credential theft, or phishing attacks. This can compromise the security and privacy of website users.
recommended_actions: Update the WordPress Contact Form Builder plugin to the latest patched version or apply any available security fixes.

title: Drupal avatar_uploader 7.x-1.0-beta8 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability that...
description: Drupal avatar_uploader 7.x-1.0-beta8 contains a reflected cross-site scripting vulnerability that allows unauthenticated attackers to inject malicious scripts by manipulating the file parameter. Attackers can craft URLs with script payloads in the file parameter of avatar_uploader.pages.inc to execute arbitrary JavaScript in victim browsers.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The reflected cross-site scripting (XSS) vulnerability in the Drupal avatar_uploader module allows attackers to execute arbitrary JavaScript in victim browsers. This can lead to session hijacking, credential theft, or the injection of malicious content, potentially compromising user accounts and sensitive data.
recommended_actions: Immediately update or disable the vulnerable avatar_uploader module if it is in use. Check for patches or newer versions that address CVE-2022-50957.
Implement input validation and sanitization for all user-supplied data, especially for the 'file' parameter, to mitigate exploitation.
Educate users and administrators about the risks of clicking on untrusted or suspicious links, and monitor for unusual activity in user accounts.

title: CVE-2026-7682 - A security flaw has been discovered in Edimax BR-6208AC 1.02. The impacted element is the function s
description: A security flaw has been discovered in Edimax BR-6208AC 1.02. The impacted element is the function setWAN of the file /goform/setWAN of the component L2TP Mode. The manipulation of the argument L2TPUserName results in command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-7682.

title: CVE-2026-5337 - During the analysis, it was identified that authenticated attackers with Subscriber-level access or
description: During the analysis, it was identified that authenticated attackers with Subscriber-level access or higher are able to perform an Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR) attack. This vulnerability exists because the Frontend File Manager Plugin WordPress plugin through 23.6 does not properly validate user authorization for the requested uploaded file when processing download requests. By modifying the value of the 'file_id' parameter in the download endpoint (e.g., http://localhost/?do=wpfm_download&file_id=40&nm_file_nonce=a36fb893f1), an attacker can access files belonging to other users, including privileged users such as administrators. This allows unauthorized access/read to sensitive data stored  within the application.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Authenticated attackers with Subscriber-level access can exploit an IDOR vulnerability in the Frontend File Manager Plugin for WordPress, potentially leading to unauthorized access to sensitive files or data.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Businesses using WordPress
recommended_actions: Update the Frontend File Manager Plugin to the latest patched version as soon as it becomes available.
Implement strict access controls and monitor user activity for suspicious behavior, especially for Subscriber-level accounts.
Review and validate all user inputs and authorization checks in custom WordPress plugins to prevent IDOR vulnerabilities.

description: A security vulnerability has been detected in jsbroks COCO Annotator up to 0.11.1. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

title: CVE-2026-5063 - The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cro
description: The NEX-Forms – Ultimate Forms Plugin for WordPress plugin for WordPress is vulnerable to Stored Cross-Site Scripting via POST parameter key names in the submit_nex_form() function in versions up to, and including, 9.1.11 due to insufficient input sanitization and output escaping. This makes it possible for unauthenticated attackers to inject arbitrary web scripts in pages that will execute whenever a user accesses an injected page.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-5063.

description: A vulnerability was determined in kerwincui FastBee up to 1.2.1. This manipulation of the argument noticeContent causes cross site scripting. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

title: CVE-2026-40561 - Starlet versions through 0.31 for Perl allows HTTP Request Smuggling via Improper Header Precedence.
description:
Starlet versions through 0.31 for Perl allows HTTP Request Smuggling via Improper Header Precedence.

Starlet incorrectly prioritizes "Content-Length" over "Transfer-Encoding: chunked" when both headers are present in an HTTP request. Per RFC 7230 3.3.3, Transfer-Encoding must take precedence.

An attacker could exploit this to smuggle malicious HTTP requests via a front-end reverse proxy.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with medium severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40561.

description: A vulnerability has been found in CodeWise Tornet Scooter Mobile App 4.75 on iOS/Android. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file /TwoFactor. The exploitability is regarded as difficult. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.
